title: Heart of Darkness
author: Conrad, Joseph
summary: Companions lounge on the Nellie, a cruising yawl anchored on the Thames, while Marlow recalls voyages and muses that the river has been a dark place. He evokes Roman voyagers, triremes and legionaries, then later traders and conquerors whose enterprise amounted to robbery and aggravated murder. Marlow reflects on the fascination and abomination of imperial ventures, on efficiency, pretence and sacrifice. Restless after service in eastern seas, he recalls a childhood passion for maps and longing for an inland river seen as a snake on the chart, leading him to seek charge of a steamboat for a Continental trading concern.

characters.name: Unnamed Narrator
characters.personality: observant, framing listener to Marlow's tale
characters.lifespan: present on the Nellie; fate unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: Marlow
characters.lifespan: survives to narrate experiences; fate beyond excerpt unspecified

characters.name: Director of Companies
characters.personality: authoritative, nautical in appearance, host and captain of the Nellie
characters.lifespan: present on the yacht; fate unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: The Lawyer
characters.personality: respectable, the 'best of old fellows', genteel
characters.lifespan: lies on the deck rug during framing scene; fate unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: The Accountant
characters.personality: meticulous, amused by dominoes, architectural in habit
characters.lifespan: present on the Nellie; fate unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: The Poor Chap
characters.personality: pitiful, encountered by Marlow later in his tale
characters.lifespan: met at the farthest point of navigation; fate unspecified in excerpt

characters.name: Commander of a Trireme (hypothetical)
characters.personality: hardy Roman commander envisaged by Marlow
characters.lifespan: imagined to have suffered disease and death in hostile climate (as Marlow suggests)

characters.name: Decent Young Citizen in a Toga (hypothetical)
characters.personality: naïve, uninitiated into wilderness mysteries in Marlow's example
characters.lifespan: imagined to feel regret and despair; fate unspecified

characters.name: Legionaries (group)
characters.personality: capable builders and fighters in Roman example
characters.lifespan: imagined participants in Roman expeditions; fate unspecified

characters.name: Prefect / Tax-gatherer / Trader (types)
characters.personality: administrators or opportunists in Marlow's illustrations
characters.lifespan: mentioned as roles; individual fates unspecified

unusual_words.definition: a small sailing vessel with two masts
unusual_words.context: The Nellie is described as a 'cruising yawl' anchored on the Thames.

unusual_words.definition: heavy device dropped to hold a vessel in place
unusual_words.context: The yawl 'swung to her anchor' and was at rest.

unusual_words.definition: the rise and fall of sea levels
unusual_words.context: The flood had made and they waited for the turn of the tide.

unusual_words.definition: the area of sea visible from shore; near offshore
unusual_words.context: In the offing the sea and the sky were welded together.

unusual_words.definition: joined seamlessly or fused together (figurative)
unusual_words.context: The sea and the sky were 'welded together without a joint'.

unusual_words.definition: emitting light; bright
unusual_words.context: The luminous space showed the tanned sails of barges.

unusual_words.definition: darkened in color, like tanned leather or skin
unusual_words.context: The tanned sails of the barges were visible in the estuary.

unusual_words.definition: large flat-bottomed boats for cargo on rivers
unusual_words.context: Tanned sails of the barges drifting up with the tide seemed to stand still.

unusual_words.definition: spars extending diagonally across a sail
unusual_words.context: Gleams of varnished sprits were noted on the barges.

unusual_words.definition: coated with a shiny protective finish
unusual_words.context: Gleams of varnished sprits were mentioned among the barges' sails.

unusual_words.definition: a slight obscuration of the lower atmosphere
unusual_words.context: A haze rested on the low shores that ran out to sea.

unusual_words.definition: wet, low-lying land with grasses
unusual_words.context: The mist on the Essex marsh was like a gauzy and radiant fabric.

unusual_words.definition: expressing sorrow or gloom
unusual_words.context: The air was dark above Gravesend and condensed into a mournful gloom.

unusual_words.definition: partial or total darkness; a somber atmosphere
unusual_words.context: A mournful gloom brooded motionless over the great town.

unusual_words.definition: the forward part of a ship
unusual_words.context: The Director stood in the bows looking to seaward.

unusual_words.definition: relating to sailors, ships, or navigation
unusual_words.context: On the whole river there was nothing that looked half so nautical.

unusual_words.definition: a seaman qualified to guide ships through difficult waters
unusual_words.context: The Director resembled a pilot, 'trustworthiness personified.'

unusual_words.definition: the quality of being reliable and dependable
unusual_words.context: The Director resembled a pilot, which to a seaman is trustworthiness personified.

unusual_words.definition: a tie or connection between people
unusual_words.context: There was the bond of the sea between the companions.

unusual_words.definition: long tales or stories, especially of seamen
unusual_words.context: The bond of the sea made them tolerant of each other's yarns.

unusual_words.definition: a soft pad for sitting
unusual_words.context: The Lawyer had the only cushion on deck and lay on the rug.

unusual_words.definition: a gaming set of small rectangular tiles
unusual_words.context: The Accountant had brought out a box of dominoes.

unusual_words.definition: in a manner related to building or structured arrangement
unusual_words.context: The Accountant was toying architecturally with the bones (dominoes).

unusual_words.definition: slang for domino tiles
unusual_words.context: Architecturally toying with the bones refers to arranging domino tiles.

unusual_words.definition: sitting with legs crossed
unusual_words.context: Marlow sat cross-legged right aft, leaning against the mizzen-mast.

unusual_words.definition: the aftmost mast on a three-masted vessel
unusual_words.context: Marlow leaned against the mizzen-mast as he sat cross-legged.

unusual_words.definition: hollowed or depressed, often describing cheeks
unusual_words.context: Marlow had sunken cheeks and a yellow complexion.

unusual_words.definition: practicing strict self-discipline and abstention
unusual_words.context: Marlow had an ascetic aspect, resembling an idol.

unusual_words.definition: an image or representation worshipped as a god; used metaphorically
unusual_words.context: With arms dropped and palms outwards he resembled an idol.

unusual_words.definition: toward the stern or rear of a vessel
unusual_words.context: Marlow sat right aft, near the stern of the yawl.

unusual_words.definition: calm and peaceful
unusual_words.context: They felt meditative and fit for nothing but placid staring.

unusual_words.definition: extremely beautiful or delicate
unusual_words.context: The day was ending in a serenity of still and exquisite brilliance.

unusual_words.definition: thin and translucent, like gauze
unusual_words.context: The mist was like a gauzy and radiant fabric draping the low shores.

unusual_words.definition: shining or glowing brightly
unusual_words.context: A gauzy and radiant fabric hung from the wooded rises inland.

unusual_words.definition: very sheer and light; almost transparent
unusual_words.context: The mist draping the low shores was described as diaphanous folds.

unusual_words.definition: dark or dull in tone; gloomy
unusual_words.context: Only the gloom to the west became more sombre every minute.

unusual_words.definition: in a way that suggests something bad will happen
unusual_words.context: The monstrous town was still marked ominously on the sky.

unusual_words.definition: very vivid in color, especially to create an unpleasant effect
unusual_words.context: A lurid glare marked the upper reaches under the stars.

unusual_words.definition: the rising tide
unusual_words.context: They waited patiently until the end of the flood.

unusual_words.definition: the receding tide
unusual_words.context: They would hear Marlow's tale before the ebb began to run.

unusual_words.definition: respected and impressive; majestic
unusual_words.context: The river was seen in the august light of abiding memories.

unusual_words.definition: deep respect or awe
unusual_words.context: A man who had 'followed the sea' with reverence could evoke the spirit of the past.

unusual_words.definition: bring or recall to the conscious mind
unusual_words.context: Nothing is easier than to evoke the great spirit of the past upon the lower reaches.

unusual_words.definition: accorded a great deal of respect because of age or dignity
unusual_words.context: They looked at the venerable stream in tranquillity.

unusual_words.definition: never stopping or pausing; continuous
unusual_words.context: The tidal current runs to and fro in its unceasing service.

unusual_words.definition: wandering knights seeking adventure
unusual_words.context: The river had borne men like knights-errant of the sea.

unusual_words.definition: round in shape; stout
unusual_words.context: From the Golden Hind returning with her rotund flanks full of treasure.

unusual_words.definition: the sides of a ship
unusual_words.context: The Golden Hind returned with her rotund flanks full of treasure.

unusual_words.definition: name of a famous ship (also mythological darkness), used here historically
unusual_words.context: Ships like the Erebus and Terror are mentioned as bound on conquests.

unusual_words.definition: name of a famous ship; paired with Erebus
unusual_words.context: The Erebus and Terror bound on other conquests—and that never returned.

unusual_words.definition: people who intrude into a place or sphere of activity
unusual_words.context: The dark 'interlopers' of the Eastern trade are mentioned among seafarers.

unusual_words.definition: people who seek risky or exciting experiences
unusual_words.context: Adventurers and the settlers had sailed from Deptford and Greenwich.

unusual_words.definition: people who establish a community in a new place
unusual_words.context: The river had borne settlers and adventurers to distant lands.

unusual_words.definition: an ancient Mediterranean warship with three banks of oars
unusual_words.context: Marlow imagines a commander of a fine trireme ordered to the north.

unusual_words.definition: ancient peoples of what is now France and surrounding regions
unusual_words.context: Running overland across the Gauls to reach the river is imagined.

unusual_words.definition: soldiers of a Roman legion
unusual_words.context: Marlow imagines putting legionaries in charge of such craft.

unusual_words.definition: a celebrated ancient Roman wine (Falernian wine)
unusual_words.context: Marlow contrasts Falernian wine with the Thames water they must drink.

unusual_words.definition: to boast about achievements
unusual_words.context: A commander might later brag about what he had gone through.

unusual_words.definition: the state of being fierce, cruel, or uncivilized
unusual_words.context: In some inland post the savagery had closed round him.

unusual_words.definition: impossible to understand or comprehend
unusual_words.context: To live amidst the incomprehensible is also detestable, Marlow says.

unusual_words.definition: deserving intense dislike
unusual_words.context: Marlow says the incomprehensible is also detestable.

unusual_words.definition: the power to attract and hold attention
unusual_words.context: There is a fascination, too, that goes to work upon him.

unusual_words.definition: a thing that causes disgust or hatred
unusual_words.context: Marlow calls it 'the fascination of the abomination.'

unusual_words.definition: to give up resistance
unusual_words.context: He imagines the growing regrets, the surrender, the hate.

unusual_words.definition: intense dislike or hostility
unusual_words.context: Marlow lists surrender and the hate growing in men in the wilderness.

unusual_words.definition: ability to accomplish a job with minimum waste
unusual_words.context: What saves us is efficiency—the devotion to efficiency.

unusual_words.definition: a crushing or constricting action; here metaphorical
unusual_words.context: Their administration was merely a squeeze, and nothing more.

unusual_words.definition: those who acquire territory by force
unusual_words.context: They were conquerors, and for that you want only brute force.

unusual_words.definition: savaged strength or force without refinement
unusual_words.context: For that you want only brute force—nothing to boast of.

unusual_words.definition: the action of taking property unlawfully
unusual_words.context: It was just robbery with violence, aggravated murder on a great scale.

unusual_words.definition: made more serious or severe
unusual_words.context: Aggravated murder on a great scale is the phrase used.

unusual_words.definition: the unlawful premeditated killing of one human by another
unusual_words.context: Marlow characterizes the enterprise as aggravated murder on a great scale.

unusual_words.definition: a concept or belief that motivates action
unusual_words.context: What redeems it is the idea only—an unselfish belief in the idea.

unusual_words.definition: free from selfishness; putting others first
unusual_words.context: An unselfish belief in the idea is what redeems conquest, Marlow says.

unusual_words.definition: an attempt to make something that is not the case appear true
unusual_words.context: Not a sentimental pretence but an idea; an unselfish belief.

unusual_words.definition: giving up something valued for the sake of something else
unusual_words.context: Something you can set up and bow down before, and offer a sacrifice to.

unusual_words.definition: visible, gaseous part of a fire
unusual_words.context: Flames glided in the river—small green flames, red flames, white flames.

unusual_words.definition: moved smoothly and quietly
unusual_words.context: Flames glided in the river, pursuing and overtaking each other.

unusual_words.definition: proper name used in the phrase 'Chapman light-house'
unusual_words.context: The Chapman light-house, a three-legged thing erect on a mud-flat, shone strongly.

unusual_words.definition: a tower with a light to warn or guide ships
unusual_words.context: The Chapman light-house is described as a three-legged thing.

unusual_words.definition: having three supports or legs
unusual_words.context: The Chapman lighthouse is called a three-legged thing on a mud-flat.

unusual_words.definition: the navigable channel for vessels
unusual_words.context: Lights of ships moved in the fairway—a great stir of lights.

unusual_words.definition: vast, unnatural, or shockingly large
unusual_words.context: The place of the monstrous town was still marked ominously on the sky.

unusual_words.definition: a strong or dazzling light
unusual_words.context: A lurid glare under the stars marked the town.

unusual_words.definition: a brief, wavering movement of light
unusual_words.context: We live in the flicker—may it last as long as the old earth keeps rolling!

unusual_words.definition: unsure or tentative in speech or action
unusual_words.context: He said, in a hesitating voice, 'I suppose you fellows remember...'

unusual_words.definition: living or operating on inland waters as opposed to the sea
unusual_words.context: Marlow says he did once turn fresh-water sailor for a bit.

unusual_words.definition: one who works aboard a ship
unusual_words.context: Marlow references turning fresh-water sailor before his river voyage.

unusual_words.definition: not leading to a firm conclusion; unresolved
unusual_words.context: Marlow calls his experiences 'inconclusive experiences.'

unusual_words.definition: reaching a highest point or climax
unusual_words.context: He refers to the culminating point of his experience at the farthest navigation.

unusual_words.definition: deserving or arousing pity
unusual_words.context: His experience was sombre enough, too—and pitiful—not extraordinary.

unusual_words.definition: an amount of something taken at one time; metaphorical here
unusual_words.context: He had a regular dose of the East—Indian Ocean, Pacific, China Seas.

unusual_words.definition: spending time in a lazy or idle way
unusual_words.context: He was loafing about, hindering you fellows in your work.

unusual_words.definition: creating difficulties that result in delay
unusual_words.context: He was hindering the others in their work while he loafed.

unusual_words.definition: entering as an intruder
unusual_words.context: He was invading their homes 'just as though I had got a heavenly mission.'

unusual_words.definition: of or like heaven; here used sarcastically
unusual_words.context: He felt as though he had a 'heavenly mission to civilize you.'

unusual_words.definition: to bring out of a savage or primitive condition
unusual_words.context: He spoke of having a heavenly mission to civilize them.

unusual_words.definition: a strong desire or craving
unusual_words.context: He had a hankering after the biggest blank space on the map.

unusual_words.definition: charts representing areas of the earth
unusual_words.context: As a child he had a passion for maps and would lose himself in them.

unusual_words.definition: here a metaphor for a river's winding shape on a map
unusual_words.context: He describes the great river as resembling 'an immense snake uncoiled.'
